units.name: Pure Mathematics 1
units.headline:
- Total formulas needed: ~55
- Given in booklet: 3 (mensuration of sphere, cone curved surface, cosine rule)
- **Must memorize: 52**
- Priority breakdown: P0=12, P1=22, P2=14, P3=4

rows.formula: $a^m \cdot a^n = a^{m+n},\quad \dfrac{a^m}{a^n} = a^{m-n},\quad (a^m)^n = a^{mn}$
rows.why: Forgot on Apr 11 Q2 (cubed 16 but not $x^{-1/2}$); used everywhere

rows.formula: $a^{-n} = \dfrac{1}{a^n},\quad \left(\dfrac{a}{b}\right)^{-n} = \left(\dfrac{b}{a}\right)^n,\quad \dfrac{1}{a^{-n}} = a^n$
$a^{1/n} = \sqrt[n]{a},\quad a^{m/n} = (\sqrt[n]{a})^m = \sqrt[n]{a^m},\quad a^0 = 1$
$(ab)^n = a^n b^n$ — every factor takes the exponent
rows.why: "Cubed 16 but forgot to cube $x^{-1/2}$" — Apr 11 Q2

rows.formula: $\sqrt{ab} = \sqrt{a}\sqrt{b},\quad \sqrt{\dfrac{a}{b}} = \dfrac{\sqrt{a}}{\sqrt{b}}$
Conjugate rationalising: $\dfrac{1}{a+\sqrt{b}} \cdot \dfrac{a - \sqrt{b}}{a - \sqrt{b}} = \dfrac{a - \sqrt{b}}{a^2 - b}$
rows.why: Apr 5 Q2 — Phuc forgot conjugate technique

rows.formula: Difference of squares: $(a+\sqrt{b})(a-\sqrt{b}) = a^2 - b$

chapters.title: 2 — Quadratics

rows.formula: $x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ (quadratic formula)
rows.why: NOT in booklet. Forgot Mar 9 Q3 — explicitly self-flagged

rows.formula: Discriminant $\Delta = b^2 - 4ac$. $\Delta > 0$: 2 distinct real; $\Delta = 0$: repeated; $\Delta < 0$: no real
rows.why: Trigger words: "tangent / single point" → $\Delta = 0$. Apr 5 Q4

rows.formula: Complete the square: $ax^2 + bx + c = a\!\left(x + \tfrac{b}{2a}\right)^2 + c - \tfrac{b^2}{4a}$
Vertex of $a(x+p)^2 + q$ is $(-p, q)$ (min if $a>0$, max if $a<0$)
Vieta: sum of roots $= -b/a$, product $= c/a$
"Split the middle term" factorising: for $ax^2 + bx + c$, find two numbers multiplying to $ac$ and summing to $b$
rows.why: Faster than the formula when factorable

chapters.in_booklet:
- *(none — quadratics formulas are entirely outside the P1 booklet)*

---

rows.formula: $y < f(x)$ ⇒ region **below** curve; $y > f(x)$ ⇒ region **above**
rows.why: Forgot in Mar 9 Q5, Apr 5 Q6(b), Apr 11 Q11(d) — 3 papers in a row

rows.formula: Strict inequality (`<`, `>`) ⇒ **dashed** boundary; non-strict (`≤`, `≥`) ⇒ **solid**
Multiple inequalities: solution is **intersection (overlap)** of individual regions
Discriminant trigger words: "meet at single point / tangent" → $\Delta = 0$; "two distinct points" → $\Delta > 0$; "doesn't intersect" → $\Delta < 0$
rows.why: Apr 5 Q4 — Phuc figured this out himself

rows.formula: For 4 distinct real roots of biquadratic $x^4 + bx^2 + c = 0$ with $u = x^2$: need $\Delta_u > 0$ AND **both** $u$-roots positive (sum > 0 AND product > 0)
rows.why: Apr 11 Q8(c) — Phuc forgot positivity check

rows.formula: $y = f(x) + a$: shift **up** by $a$
$y = f(x + a)$: shift **left** by $a$ (inside, opposite direction)
$y = af(x)$: vertical stretch SF $a$ (outside, intuitive)
$y = f(ax)$: horizontal compress SF $\tfrac{1}{a}$ (inside, reciprocal)
$y = -f(x)$: reflect in $x$-axis. $y = f(-x)$: reflect in $y$-axis
"Inside" $f()$ → horizontal & **opposite** intuition; "outside" $f()$ → vertical & matches intuition
Number of intersections of $y = f(x)$ and $y = g(x)$ = number of real solutions of $f(x) = g(x)$
rows.why: Apr 11 Q4(d) — interpret graph, no algebra

rows.formula: "Touches the axis" = repeated root; preserved under transformation
Gradient: $m = \dfrac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$
Point-slope: $y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$
Midpoint: $M = \left(\tfrac{x_1+x_2}{2},\ \tfrac{y_1+y_2}{2}\right)$
Parallel: $m_1 = m_2$. Perpendicular: $m_1 m_2 = -1$
Line parallel to $x$-axis through $(a, b)$: $y = b$. Parallel to $y$-axis: $x = a$
For $y = mx + c$: gradient $= m$ (the coefficient), $y$-intercept $= c$
rows.why: Mar 9 Q4 — Phuc confused gradient with whole line equation

chapters.title: 6 — Trigonometric Ratios (sine/cosine rules, area)

rows.formula: Sine Rule: $\dfrac{a}{\sin A} = \dfrac{b}{\sin B} = \dfrac{c}{\sin C}$
rows.why: NOT in booklet. **Forgot every paper.** Mar 9, Apr 5, Apr 6, Apr 11 — 4/4

rows.formula: Area of triangle (SAS): $\text{Area} = \tfrac{1}{2} ab \sin C$
rows.why: Apr 5 Q5, Apr 6 Q2 — chronic recall failure

rows.formula: $\sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1$
rows.why: Apr 6 Q2 — used to get $\cos\theta$ from $\sin\theta$

rows.formula: $\tan \theta = \dfrac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}$
Obtuse angle ($90° < \theta < 180°$) → $\cos\theta < 0$
rows.why: Apr 6 Q2 — sign of cosine for obtuse

rows.formula: Exact values: $\sin/\cos/\tan$ at $0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°$
$\sin\theta = 1$ at $\theta = \tfrac{\pi}{2} + 2n\pi$ (max); $\sin\theta = -1$ at $\theta = \tfrac{3\pi}{2} + 2n\pi$ (min)
$\cos\theta = 1$ at $\theta = 2n\pi$; $\cos\theta = -1$ at $\theta = \pi + 2n\pi$
Period of $y = A\sin(Bx + C)$ is $\dfrac{360°}{
$\sin(180° - \theta) = \sin\theta$; $\cos(180° - \theta) = -\cos\theta$; $\sin(360° - \theta) = -\sin\theta$; $\cos(360° - \theta) = \cos\theta$
rows.why: Quadrant symmetries for solving trig equations

chapters.in_booklet:
- **Cosine rule**: $a^2 = b^2 + c^2 - 2bc\cos A$ — given. Use with **two sides + included angle** OR **three sides** to find an angle.
- *(Phuc still forgets to LOOK IT UP — habit issue; treat as effectively must-memorize)*

---

rows.formula: $2\pi \text{ rad} = 360°$, $\pi \text{ rad} = 180°$, $1 \text{ rad} = \tfrac{180°}{\pi}$
Arc length: $\ell = r\theta$ (θ in **radians**)
rows.why: Mar 9 Q8, Apr 6 Q8 — forgot every time

rows.formula: Sector area: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} r^2 \theta$
rows.why: Mar 9 Q8, Apr 5 Q5, Apr 6 Q8 — 3 papers

rows.formula: Segment area: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} r^2 (\theta - \sin\theta)$
Major angle = $2\pi$ − minor angle; major sector = bigger slice
Angles on a straight line sum to $\pi$ rad ($180°$); angles in a triangle sum to $\pi$ rad
rows.why: Apr 5 Q5(b) — needed to deduce angle ABE

rows.formula: In right triangle with hypotenuse $h$ and angle $\theta$: adjacent $= h\cos\theta$, opposite $= h\sin\theta$
Power rule: $\dfrac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1}$
$\dfrac{d}{dx}(c) = 0$, $\dfrac{d}{dx}(kf(x)) = kf'(x)$, sum/difference linearity
Tangent at $(a, f(a))$: $y - f(a) = f'(a)(x - a)$
Normal at $(a, f(a))$: $y - f(a) = -\dfrac{1}{f'(a)}(x - a)$
$\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ at the tangent point = **gradient** $m$ of the tangent line, NOT the whole equation $y = mx + c$
rows.why: Mar 9 Q4 — Phuc set $\frac{dy}{dx}$ equal to whole line

rows.formula: Stationary points: solve $f'(x) = 0$
Second derivative test: $f''(a) > 0$ → local min; $f''(a) < 0$ → local max; $f''(a) = 0$ → inconclusive
Two-unknowns trap: when integrating $f'$ gives $C$ alongside an unknown $k$, first use a separate condition (e.g. $f''(P) = 0$) involving ONLY $k$, THEN integrate and use $P$ to find $C$
$\displaystyle \int x^n\,dx = \dfrac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + C$ for $n \neq -1$
$\displaystyle \int kf(x)\,dx = k \int f(x)\,dx$; integral of sum = sum of integrals
$\dfrac{1}{x^n} = x^{-n}$ — **rewrite as negative power BEFORE integrating**
rows.why: Apr 5 Q1 — Phuc didn't know how to integrate $\frac{3}{2x^3}$

rows.formula: For fractions like $\dfrac{ax^p + bx^q}{x^r}$: divide each term by $x^r$, then integrate
Always add $+C$ for indefinite integrals
Definite: $\displaystyle \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = F(b) - F(a)$ where $F' = f$. **No $+C$.**
Use a given point $(a, f(a))$ on the curve to find $C$ after integrating $f'$
Area between curve and $x$-axis: $\displaystyle \int_a^b y\,dx$ (signed; below x-axis → negative)

chapters.title: (cross-cutting) — Mensuration
chapters.in_booklet:
- **Surface area of sphere** = $4\pi r^2$
- **Curved surface area of cone** = $\pi r \times \text{slant height}$

(Both given explicitly. The student should NOT memorize these but must know to use them and recognise when a question demands them.)

Other mensuration NOT given (you DO need to know):
- Volume of sphere = $\tfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$
- Volume of cone = $\tfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$
- Volume of cylinder = $\pi r^2 h$
- Surface area of cylinder = $2\pi r^2 + 2\pi r h$
- Pythagoras: $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$ (right-angled triangles)

---

units.top10:
1. **Sine Rule** $\dfrac{a}{\sin A} = \dfrac{b}{\sin B} = \dfrac{c}{\sin C}$ — forgotten in **all 4 papers**. Drill until automatic.
2. **Area (SAS)** $\text{Area} = \tfrac{1}{2}ab\sin C$ — companion to sine rule, also forgotten 4×.
3. **Sector area** $A = \tfrac{1}{2}r^2\theta$ and **arc length** $\ell = r\theta$ (radians) — forgotten 3× across radians questions.
4. **Quadratic formula** $x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ — NOT in booklet; Phuc explicitly forgot Mar 9 Q3.
5. **Discriminant trigger words** $\Delta = b^2 - 4ac$: tangent → $\Delta = 0$, two roots → $\Delta > 0$, no roots → $\Delta < 0$.
6. **Region inequality direction**: $y < f(x)$ = below curve; $y > f(x)$ = above; pick a sample point. Failed 3×.
7. **Index laws** especially $(ab)^n = a^n b^n$ (every factor takes the power) and $\left(\tfrac{a}{b}\right)^{-n} = \left(\tfrac{b}{a}\right)^n$.
8. **Graph transformation rules**: inside $f()$ ↔ horizontal & opposite; outside ↔ vertical & intuitive.
9. **Power rule both ways**: $\dfrac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1}$ and $\int x^n\,dx = \dfrac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} + C$. Rewrite $\dfrac{1}{x^n}$ as $x^{-n}$ FIRST.
10. **Tangent gradient = $m$ only**: $\dfrac{dy}{dx}\big|_P$ equals the slope of the tangent line, NOT the whole line equation $mx + c$. Mar 9 Q4 trap.

units.name: Pure Mathematics 2
units.headline:
- Total formulas needed: ~60 (P2-specific) + P1 inheritance
- Given in booklet for P2: 5 (arithmetic series, geometric series, log change-of-base, binomial, trapezium rule)
- **Must memorize (P2-specific): 55**
- Priority breakdown: P0=18, P1=24, P2=10, P3=3

rows.formula: **Factor theorem**: $(x - c)$ is a factor of $f(x)$ iff $f(c) = 0$
rows.why: Mar 9 Q4, Apr 6 Q4, Apr 12 Q4, Apr 19 Q2 — every paper

rows.formula: **Remainder theorem**: $f(x) \div (x - c)$ leaves remainder $f(c)$
Generalised: $(ax - b)$ is a factor iff $f\!\left(\tfrac{b}{a}\right) = 0$
rows.why: Apr 12 Q4 used $f(-\tfrac{1}{2})$ for $(2x+1)$; Apr 19 Q2 used $f(\tfrac{3}{2})$ for $(2x-3)$

rows.formula: "Show only one real root": divide out the known linear factor → discriminant of quadratic factor < 0
rows.why: Apr 19 Q2 — Phuc's instinct was wrong (tried calculator factor)

rows.formula: Polynomial long division setup: divide leading term, multiply back, **subtract**, bring down next
Sign trap when subtracting: $-(-6x^2) = +6x^2$

chapters.title: 2 — Coordinate Geometry in $x,y$ (circles)

rows.formula: **Circle (centre-radius form)**: $(x - a)^2 + (y - b)^2 = r^2$, centre $(a, b)$, radius $r$
rows.why: NOT in booklet. Forgot Mar 9, Apr 5, Apr 6 — 3 papers; Apr 19 fine

rows.formula: Circle (general form): $x^2 + y^2 + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0$ → centre $(-g, -f)$, radius $\sqrt{g^2 + f^2 - c}$
Tangent ⟂ radius at point of contact: $m_{\text{tangent}} \cdot m_{\text{radius}} = -1$
rows.why: Apr 19 Q3 — Mr Fuat's go-to method

rows.formula: Angle in semicircle = $90°$ (Thales): if $AB$ is diameter and $P$ on circle, $\angle APB = 90°$
Perpendicular from centre to chord bisects the chord
Chord parallel to axis ⇒ equation is $y = k$ (or $x = k$); only need to find the constant
rows.why: Apr 5 Q3 — Phuc completely missed this

rows.formula: Chord length parallel to $x$-axis at $y = k$ on circle $(x-a)^2 + (y-b)^2 = r^2$ is $2\sqrt{r^2 - (k-b)^2}$

chapters.in_booklet:
- *(Circle formulas not in booklet for P2)*

---

chapters.title: 3 — Exponentials and Logarithms

rows.formula: $\log_a(xy) = \log_a x + \log_a y$
rows.why: Apr 19 Q5 — Phuc wrote $\log A + \log B = \log(A+B)$ — wrong

rows.formula: $\log_a\!\left(\tfrac{x}{y}\right) = \log_a x - \log_a y$
$\log_a(x^n) = n\log_a x$ — **coefficient goes INTO the power**
rows.why: Apr 6 Q6, Apr 19 — confused $2\log x$ with $\log^2 x$

rows.formula: $\log_a a = 1,\quad \log_a 1 = 0,\quad \log_a a^k = k$
$\log_a A = B \iff A = a^B$ — only when log has **coefficient 1**
rows.why: Apr 6 Q6a — Phuc missed bringing 2 into power

rows.formula: Constant-to-log trick: $k = \log_a(a^k)$ — e.g. $4 = \log_3 81 = \log_2 16$, $\tfrac{1}{2} = \log_4 2$
$\log A + \log B \neq \log(A + B)$ — it's $\log(AB)$ (product, not sum)
rows.why: Apr 19 Q5 — single most-flagged log error

rows.formula: $a^x = b \Rightarrow x = \dfrac{\log b}{\log a} = \dfrac{\ln b}{\ln a}$
rows.why: Apr 6 Q7, Apr 19 Q6c — exceeds-3-million; constant tool

rows.formula: $e^x > 0$ for all $x$; $a^x > 0$ for $a > 0$ — exponentials never zero
**Domain check**: arguments of every $\log$ must be $> 0$. ALWAYS verify final solutions
Distinction: $2\log x = \log(x^2)$, but $\log^2 x = (\log x)^2$ — different objects
$y = a^x$ graph: passes through $(0, 1)$, asymptote $y = 0$, increasing for $a > 1$
Modelling: "same amount" → linear ($y = mx + c$); "same percentage" → exponential ($y = ab^t$ or $ae^{kt}$)
rows.why: Apr 6 Q7 — confused linear vs exponential

chapters.in_booklet:
- **Change of base**: $\log_a x = \dfrac{\log_b x}{\log_b a}$ — given. Use to compute $\log_3 70$ via natural logs.

---

chapters.title: 4 — Binomial Expansion (positive integer index)

rows.formula: $\binom{n}{r} = \dfrac{n!}{r!(n-r)!} = {}^nC_r$
Symmetry: $\binom{n}{r} = \binom{n}{n-r}$
Pascal pattern: $\binom{n}{0} = \binom{n}{n} = 1$, $\binom{n}{1} = n$
Mechanic: $(a + bx)^n$ — first-term power **decreases** $a^n, a^{n-1}, \ldots$; $bx$ power **increases**; coefficients are $\binom{n}{r}$
rows.why: Apr 5 Q2, Apr 6 Q4, Apr 19 Q4 — chronic recall failure

rows.formula: $(bx)^r = b^r x^r$ — both base AND variable take the power
rows.why: Apr 5 Q2 — Phuc forgot the constant power

rows.formula: "Coefficient of $x^r$" = the numerical coefficient (no $x$) of that specific term: $\binom{n}{r} a^{n-r} b^r$
rows.why: Apr 5 Q2 — Phuc's general term setup

rows.formula: Sign condition: when $p$ has two values from $p^2 = k$ (giving $p = \pm\sqrt{k}$), use the given inequality to pick

chapters.in_booklet:
- **Binomial (positive integer $n$)**: $(a + b)^n = a^n + \binom{n}{1}a^{n-1}b + \binom{n}{2}a^{n-2}b^2 + \cdots + b^n$ — given.
- *(Phuc still re-derives from scratch every time. **Open the booklet.**)*

---

chapters.title: 5 — Sequences and Series

rows.formula: **GP common ratio test**: $\dfrac{u_2}{u_1} = \dfrac{u_3}{u_2}$ — defines a geometric sequence
"Periodic sequence" $u_{n+k} = u_n$ — write out terms, find cycle
rows.why: Apr 5 Q5, Apr 12 Q2 — flashcards-needed pattern

rows.formula: "Order" of periodic sequence = cycle length $k$
Sum of periodic: $\sum_{r=1}^{N} u_r$ = (full cycles) × (cycle sum) + (leftover terms). Use $N \bmod k$
$u_n = u_{n \bmod k}$ when $n \bmod k \neq 0$, else $u_k$. Caution off-by-one
Modelling: $t = 0$ vs $t = 1$ — read carefully ("year 1 = $n=1$" means 11 increments to year 12)
rows.why: Apr 6 Q7 — off-by-one on linear gradient

rows.formula: Recurrence $u_{n+1} = b - au_n$: build $u_2, u_3$ algebraically before substituting given relations
rows.why: Apr 19 Q11 — substitute $b = a + 9$ AFTER summing

rows.formula: "Larger / smaller possible value" of something derived from a quadratic ⇒ compute both roots, plug each in, pick
$\sum$ with small upper limit (e.g. $\sum_{n=1}^{3}$) ⇒ just add the listed terms; no series formula needed

chapters.in_booklet:
- **Arithmetic** $u_n = a + (n-1)d$, $S_n = \tfrac{n}{2}[2a + (n-1)d] = \tfrac{n}{2}(a + \ell)$ — **given**.
- **Geometric** $u_n = ar^{n-1}$, $S_n = \dfrac{a(1 - r^n)}{1 - r} = \dfrac{a(r^n - 1)}{r - 1}$ — **given**.
- **Sum to infinity** $S_\infty = \dfrac{a}{1 - r}$ for $|r| < 1$ — **given**.

> **Phuc behaviour note**: forgets these every paper despite being in the booklet. The fix is **habit**: at the start of each P2 paper, flip to the booklet page and bookmark it. He must train himself to look up rather than recall.

---

chapters.title: 6 — Trig Identities and Equations

rows.formula: $\sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1$
rows.why: NOT in booklet. Used in every trig question

rows.formula: $\tan\theta = \dfrac{\sin\theta}{\cos\theta}$
rows.why: Apr 12 Q3, Apr 19 Q9 — factor common term

rows.formula: "Factor out": when both $\sin\theta$ and $\tan\theta$ in equation → $\sin\theta\!\left(\tfrac{2}{\cos\theta} \pm 3\right) = 0$
$\sin\theta = 0$ ⇒ $\theta = 0°, 180°, 360°, \ldots$ (or $n\pi$ in radians)
rows.why: Apr 12 Q3 — Case 1 of factored equation

rows.formula: **General solution $\sin\theta = k$**: $\theta = \alpha + 360°n$ OR $\theta = (180° - \alpha) + 360°n$ where $\alpha = \sin^{-1}(k)$
**General solution $\cos\theta = k$**: $\theta = \pm\alpha + 360°n$ where $\alpha = \cos^{-1}(k)$
**General solution $\tan\theta = k$**: $\theta = \alpha + 180°n$ (period $180°$, single family)
rows.why: Apr 19 Q9 — different period from sin/cos

rows.formula: **Interval scaling for $\cos(nx)$ etc**: multiply the domain by $n$ first, find ALL solutions, divide by $n$
Quadratic-in-trig: substitute $u = \cos\theta$ (or $\sin$, $\tan$), solve quadratic, then back-substitute
Reject any $u$ value with $
"Smallest positive" trig answer: take the smallest $\theta$ value, work back to $x$
rows.why: Apr 12 Q3(b) — Phuc used $180°$ first instead of $48.2°$

rows.formula: $\sin\theta$ has period $360°$; $\cos\theta$ has period $360°$; $\tan\theta$ has period $180°$
Radians vs degrees check: look for $°$ symbol IN the equation (e.g. $2x + 40°$ ⇒ degrees mode)
rows.why: Apr 5 Q8 — wrong calculator mode trap

chapters.in_booklet:
- *(No trig identities in P2 booklet — the booklet's trig section starts in P3)*

---

rows.formula: **Product rule**: $(uv)' = u'v + uv'$
rows.why: Apr 19 Q7 — Phuc needed tutor prompt to even start

rows.formula: **Chain rule**: $\dfrac{d}{dx}(f(g(x))) = f'(g(x)) \cdot g'(x)$
Rewrite $\sqrt{x}$ as $x^{1/2}$ BEFORE differentiating
rows.why: Apr 19 Q7 — clean derivative of $\sqrt{t}\cdot p(t)$

rows.formula: Clear $x^{-1/2}$ terms: multiply equation by $x^{1/2}$ to remove fractional powers
rows.why: Apr 19 Q7 — get to clean polynomial in $t$

rows.formula: "Stationary value" condition: $\dfrac{dy}{dx} = 0$, then solve
rows.why: Apr 19 Q7 — Phuc forgot the basic rule

rows.formula: "Show maximum" via $\dfrac{d^2y}{dx^2}\bigg

chapters.title: 8 — Integration (P2 extensions: trapezium, areas)

rows.formula: Strip width: $h = \dfrac{b - a}{n}$, where $n$ = **number of strips** (NOT ordinates)
Number of ordinates = $n + 1$ (one more than strips)
Area between curve and line: $\displaystyle \int_a^b (\text{upper} - \text{lower})\,dx$
Trapezium area between two horizontal-ish ordinates at $x = a, x = b$: $\tfrac{1}{2}(y_a + y_b)(b - a)$
rows.why: Apr 19 Q10(c) — line area as trapezium

rows.formula: "Total integral = 0" when regions above & below x-axis are equal in area
"Show $\int = ak^2 + bk + c$" ⇒ compute integral fully; **match coefficients**, do NOT set integral $= 0$
rows.why: Apr 19 Q10(a) — Phuc's Q!!! Critical insight

rows.formula: Splitting $\dfrac{(\text{poly in }x)}{x^{1/2}}$: divide each term by $x^{1/2}$ to get $x^{p-1/2}$ powers, then integrate
Substitution-symmetry: $\int_{-b}^{-a} f(-x)\,dx = \int_a^b f(u)\,du$ (let $u = -x$)
Constant shift: $\int (f(x) + c)\,dx = \int f(x)\,dx + c(b - a)$ over $[a, b]$

chapters.in_booklet:
- **Trapezium rule**: $\displaystyle \int_a^b y\,dx \approx \tfrac{h}{2}\big[y_0 + y_n + 2(y_1 + y_2 + \cdots + y_{n-1})\big]$ where $h = \tfrac{b-a}{n}$ — **given**.

> **Phuc behaviour note**: forgot trapezium rule in **4 papers in a row** despite it being in the booklet. The instruction is exam-strategy, not memorization: **flip to the booklet at the start, locate the formula, leave it open**. The formula is given. Habit > recall.

---

rows.formula: **Proof by exhaustion**
rows.priority: Finite case set (e.g. even/odd, mod 4) — test every case, write conclusion sentence

rows.formula: **Proof by deduction**
rows.priority: Manipulate algebra/identities to derive RHS from LHS

rows.formula: **Disproof by counter example**
rows.priority: "Find ONE case that fails" — then state explicitly

rows.formula: "Not multiple of 4": split as $n = 2k$ (write as $2 \times$ odd) and $n = 2k+1$ (write as $4(\text{integer}) - 1$)
"Not prime": find a factor between 1 and the number itself
rows.why: Apr 6 Q8(i), Apr 19 Q8 — keep trying values

rows.formula: Even integer: $2k$. Odd integer: $2k + 1$. Consecutive integers: $n, n+1$
Always close with explicit conclusion: "Hence by exhaustion / counter example, …"

units.top10:
1. **OPEN THE BOOKLET HABIT.** Trapezium rule, binomial $(a+b)^n$, arithmetic $S_n$, geometric $S_n$, $S_\infty$, $u_n$ for AP/GP — **all given**. Phuc forgets these every paper because he doesn't open the page. Train: flip to the page in the first 30 seconds of the exam.
2. **Circle equation** $(x-a)^2 + (y-b)^2 = r^2$ — NOT in booklet. Forgot 3× consecutively. Use distance from centre for $r$.
3. **Log laws** especially $\log A + \log B = \log(AB)$ NOT $\log(A+B)$, and $n\log x = \log(x^n)$ — confused $2\log x$ with $\log^2 x$.
4. **Constant-to-log conversion** $k = \log_a(a^k)$ — vital for solving any "show that…" log equation.
5. **Domain check on logs**: every log argument must be $> 0$. Always verify final solutions.
6. **Factor theorem mechanics**: $(ax - b)$ is factor ⇔ $f(b/a) = 0$. For "only one real root": divide out, then $\Delta < 0$ on the quadratic.
7. **Trig general solutions**: $\sin\theta = k \to \alpha$ AND $180° - \alpha$; $\cos\theta = k \to \pm\alpha$; $\tan\theta = k \to \alpha$ only (period $180°$).
8. **Interval scaling** for $\cos(2x), \tan(3x)$: multiply domain by the coefficient FIRST, solve in the wider interval, then divide.
9. **Pythagorean identity** $\sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1$ and $\tan\theta = \tfrac{\sin}{\cos}$ — NOT in booklet. Foundation of every trig manipulation in P2.
10. **Modelling keywords**: "same amount" = linear/arithmetic, "same percentage" = exponential/geometric. Apr 6 Q7 confusion.

units.name: Statistics 1
units.headline:
- Total formulas needed: ~38
- Given in booklet: 9 (probability rules, $S_{xx},S_{yy},S_{xy}$, PMCC $r$, regression $a, b$, DRV expectation/variance, Normal pdf, normal tables, percentage points)
- **Must memorize: 29**
- Priority breakdown: P0=12, P1=11, P2=5, P3=1

chapters.title: 1 — Mathematical Modelling in Statistics

rows.formula: Modelling cycle (7 stages): recognise problem → devise model → predict → collect data → compare → test → refine
Advantages: quick, cheap, simplifies, predicts. Disadvantages: simplification, limited validity

chapters.in_booklet:
- (none)

---

chapters.title: 2 — Measures of Location and Spread 🔴

rows.formula: Mean (raw): $\bar{x} = \dfrac{\sum x}{n}$; (frequency table): $\bar{x} = \dfrac{\sum fx}{\sum f}$
**Variance (raw)**: $\sigma^2 = \dfrac{\sum x^2}{n} - \bar{x}^2$ — 🔴 **NOT in booklet** for raw data; Phuc's flagged gotcha
**Variance (frequency table)**: $\sigma^2 = \dfrac{\sum fx^2}{\sum f} - \bar{x}^2$
Standard deviation: $\sigma = \sqrt{\sigma^2}$
$S_{xx}$ identity: $S_{xx} = \sum(x-\bar x)^2 = \sum x^2 - \dfrac{(\sum x)^2}{n}$ — used for variance via $\sigma^2 = S_{xx}/n$
$\sum(x^2) \ne (\sum x)^2$: square then sum vs sum then square — **#1 S1 error**
**Coding**: $y = \dfrac{x-a}{b} \Rightarrow \bar{x} = b\bar{y} + a$
**Coding (variance)**: $\text{Var}(x) = b^2 \text{Var}(y)$, $\sigma_x = \
rows.why: \sigma_y$ — the $a$ shift does NOT affect spread

rows.formula: Median (list, $n$ data): position $(n+1)/2$ — if integer, that's median; else interpolate between adjacent
Median (grouped data, linear interpolation): $\text{median} = L + \dfrac{n/2 - F}{f}\cdot c$ (use $n/2$, NOT $(n+1)/2$, for grouped)
Quartile positions (list/raw discrete): $Q_1$ at $n/4$, $Q_3$ at $3n/4$ — if integer, average adjacent; if not, **round UP**
Mode = most frequent value (or modal class for grouped)
Range = max − min (basic spread)

chapters.in_booklet:
- (none — S1 booklet has nothing on basic measures)

chapters.title: 3 — Representations of Data 🔴

rows.formula: **Outlier fences**: lower = $Q_1 - k\cdot\text{IQR}$, upper = $Q_3 + k\cdot\text{IQR}$ (default $k=1.5$ unless stated)
Box plot: 5-number summary (min non-outlier, $Q_1$, median, $Q_3$, max non-outlier) + outliers as $\times$
**Stem-and-leaf**: stems vertical ascending, leaves ordered ascending; **include a KEY** (e.g. "3\
rows.hits: 🔴 Apr 2024 Q1 — gap topic for Phuc

rows.formula: **Back-to-back stem-and-leaf**: LEFT side reads RIGHT-TO-LEFT (smallest leaf next to stem); needs TWO keys
**Histogram**: area $\propto$ frequency; frequency density $= \dfrac{\text{frequency}}{\text{class width}}$
Histogram on page (cm): area-per-unit-frequency from a reference bar; new bar area = (freq ratio) × ref area
**Skewness via quartiles**: positive if $Q_3-Q_2 > Q_2-Q_1$; negative if reversed; symmetric if equal
**Skewness formula**: $\text{skew} = \dfrac{3(\bar x - \text{median})}{\sigma}$ (positive ⇒ right skew, negative ⇒ left skew)
Comparing data sets: ALWAYS one location measure + one spread measure, **in context** (with units)
"Box plot unchanged" ⇒ all 5 numbers (min, $Q_1$, median, $Q_3$, max) preserved when adding values

chapters.in_booklet:
- (none — Chapter 3 has no booklet support)

chapters.title: 4 — Probability

rows.formula: Independence: $P(A\cap B) = P(A)\cdot P(B)$ ⇔ $P(A\
Mutually exclusive: $P(A\cap B) = 0$, so $P(A\cup B) = P(A) + P(B)$
Complementary: $P(A') = 1 - P(A)$
Tree diagram: multiply along branches, add across
Venn 3-circle: 8 regions sum to 1 (incl. "outside all" region)
Population scaling: if $P(\text{event}) = \pi$ and observed count is $n$, total population $\approx n/\pi$
$A \subset B \Rightarrow P(A\cap B) = P(A)$, used in conditional ratios

chapters.in_booklet:
- $P(A\cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A\cap B)$ — given
- $P(A\cap B) = P(A)P(B\|A)$ — given
- $P(A\|B) = \dfrac{P(B\|A)P(A)}{P(B\|A)P(A) + P(B\|A')P(A')}$ — given (Bayes-style)

---

chapters.title: 5 — Correlation and Regression

rows.formula: PMCC interpretation requires ALL FOUR: direction (+/−), strength (strong/moderate/weak), the word **linear**, and **context**
**Coding invariance for $r$**: PMCC unchanged by any linear coding $y=(x-a)/b$ (and the same on the other variable)
Regression line interpretation: gradient $b$ = "for each 1 unit increase in $x$, $y$ changes by $b$ units (in context, with units)"
Extrapolation warning: regression line is only reliable WITHIN the data range; outside is unreliable
Hypothesis test for PMCC: $H_0: \rho=0$ vs $H_1: \rho > 0$ / $<0$ / $\ne 0$; reject $H_0$ if $\
Two-tailed test at significance $\alpha$ ⇒ use the **$\alpha/2$ critical-value column**; one-tailed ⇒ $\alpha$ column
NEVER write "accept $H_0$" — write "insufficient evidence to reject $H_0$"
Predict $y$: substitute $x$ into regression; **check x is in original sample range**
$-1 \le r \le 1$; $r=\pm 1$ ⇒ perfect linear; $r=0$ ⇒ no linear correlation

chapters.in_booklet:
- $S_{xx} = \sum(x_i - \bar x)^2 = \sum x_i^2 - \dfrac{(\sum x_i)^2}{n}$ — given
- $S_{yy}, S_{xy}$ analogues — given
- $r = \dfrac{S_{xy}}{\sqrt{S_{xx}S_{yy}}}$ — given
- Regression $y$ on $x$: $b = \dfrac{S_{xy}}{S_{xx}}$, $a = \bar y - b\bar x$, $y = a+bx$ — given

---

chapters.title: 6 — Discrete Random Variables

rows.formula: Probability distribution must satisfy: $\sum P(X=x_i) = 1$ (often used to find unknown $k$)
rows.why: Standard opening of every DRV Q

rows.formula: $E(X^2) = \sum x^2 P(X=x_i)$ — square FIRST, then multiply by probability
**$\text{Var}(X) = E(X^2) - [E(X)]^2$** (memorize the formula form, even though booklet gives $E((X-\mu)^2)$)
**$\text{Var}(aX+b) = a^2\,\text{Var}(X)$** — the $b$ disappears, the $a$ is squared
$E(g(X)) = \sum g(x_i) P(X=x_i)$ for any function $g$
Independent spinners/dice: $P(\text{event involving both}) = $ condition on one, sum over its distribution; or use joint prob $P(X_1=a) P(X_2=b)$
"Higher score wins" ⇒ use STRICT inequality $X_1 > X_2$ (not $\ge$)
CDF: $F(x) = P(X \le x)$ — non-decreasing step function from 0 to 1
Discrete uniform: $P(X=x) = 1/n$ for $n$ equally likely values

chapters.in_booklet:
- $E(X) = \mu = \sum x_i P(X=x_i)$ — given
- $\text{Var}(X) = \sigma^2 = \sum (x_i-\mu)^2 P(X=x_i) = \sum x_i^2 P(X=x_i) - \mu^2$ — given
- $E(g(X)) = \sum g(x_i) P(X=x_i)$ — given (still memorize for fluency)

---

chapters.title: 7 — The Normal Distribution

rows.formula: **Standardise**: $Z = \dfrac{X-\mu}{\sigma} \sim N(0,1)$
$P(X<a) = P\!\left(Z < \dfrac{a-\mu}{\sigma}\right) = \Phi(z)$ — read from booklet table
Symmetry: $P(Z < -z) = 1 - P(Z<z) = 1-\Phi(z)$
$P(a<X<b) = \Phi\!\left(\dfrac{b-\mu}{\sigma}\right) - \Phi\!\left(\dfrac{a-\mu}{\sigma}\right)$
**Inverse Normal**: given $P(X<a) = p$, look up $z$ from percentage-points table (booklet) ⇒ $a = \mu + z\sigma$
Symmetric interval: $P(\mu - k < X < \mu+k) = 0.95 \Rightarrow k = 1.96\sigma$; $=0.99 \Rightarrow k = 2.5758\sigma$
Useful percentage points (from booklet, but memorize the common ones): $z(0.05)=1.6449$, $z(0.025)=1.96$, $z(0.01)=2.3263$, $z(0.005)=2.5758$
rows.why: Faster than looking up every time

rows.formula: Conditional $P(A\
rows.priority: B) = P(A\cap B)/P(B)$; if $A\subset B$ then $P(A\cap B) = P(A)$

rows.formula: Two unknowns ($\mu$ and $\sigma$): set up TWO equations from two probabilities and solve simultaneously
"Top $p$%" / "fastest $p$%" / "longest $p$%" — translate carefully into $P(X<\cdot)$ or $P(X>\cdot)$ before standardising
Mean = median = mode for Normal (symmetric about $\mu$)

chapters.in_booklet:
- Normal pdf $\dfrac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}e^{-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}\right)^2}$ (you'll never need to compute it by hand) — given
- $\Phi(z)$ table for $z \ge 0$ — given
- Percentage points table (gives $z$ for $P(Z>z)=p$) — given

---

units.top10:
1. **Variance (raw data)**: $\sigma^2 = \dfrac{\sum x^2}{n} - \bar{x}^2$. 🔴 Phuc's flagged "NOT in booklet" gotcha.
2. **Coding rules**: $\bar x = b\bar y + a$, $\text{Var}(x) = b^2\,\text{Var}(y)$, $\sigma_x = \|b\|\sigma_y$. The $a$ shifts but doesn't scale. 🔴
3. **DRV linear**: $E(aX+b) = aE(X)+b$, $\text{Var}(aX+b) = a^2\text{Var}(X)$. The $b$ disappears, $a$ is squared.
4. **Var$(X) = E(X^2) - [E(X)]^2$**, with $E(X^2) = \sum x^2 P(X=x)$ (square FIRST).
5. **Standardise + Normal**: $Z = (X-\mu)/\sigma$, and the symmetric interval shortcut $P(\mu-k<X<\mu+k)=0.95 \Rightarrow k=1.96\sigma$.
6. **Stem-and-leaf back-to-back**: LEFT reads RIGHT-TO-LEFT, smallest next to stem; TWO keys. 🔴
7. **Quartile positions for discrete/stem-and-leaf**: $n/4$ and $3n/4$ — integer ⇒ avg adjacent, non-integer ⇒ round UP.
8. **Median (grouped, linear interp)**: $L + \dfrac{n/2 - F}{f}\cdot c$. Use $n/2$, NOT $(n+1)/2$.
9. **Outlier fences**: $Q_1 - 1.5\cdot\text{IQR}$ and $Q_3 + 1.5\cdot\text{IQR}$ (or whatever $k$ stated).
10. **PMCC interpretation 4-pack** (direction + strength + LINEAR + context) and never "accept $H_0$".

units.name: Mechanics 1
units.headline:
- Total formulas needed: ~55
- Given in booklet: 0 (M1 has nothing)
- **Must memorize: 55**
- Priority breakdown: P0=22, P1=20, P2=10, P3=3

rows.formula: **Modelling assumptions vocabulary**: light (mass=0), inextensible (no stretch ⇒ same a), smooth (no friction), rough (μR friction), particle (point mass, no rotation/air resistance), uniform (CoM at centre), peg (point), rigid (no bending)
SI base units: kg, m, s. Derived: N = kg·m/s², J = N·m, W = J/s
Light string ⇒ tension same throughout; light pulley smooth ⇒ tension same both sides
$v = u + at$
$s = ut + \tfrac{1}{2}at^2$
$s = vt - \tfrac{1}{2}at^2$

rows.why: Use when $t$ unknown — **Phuc's two-phase parachute Q5**

rows.formula: $s = \tfrac{1}{2}(u+v)t$
**Two-phase motion**: end conditions of phase 1 = initial conditions of phase 2; recompute $a$ via $F=ma$ when forces change
Vertical motion under gravity: take $a = -g$ if up positive (or $a=+g$ if down positive); $g=9.8$ m/s²
At max height, $v=0$ (vertical only); for projectiles $v_y=0$ but $v_x = u\cos\theta \ne 0$
Velocity-time graph: gradient = $a$, area under = displacement
Displacement-time graph: gradient = velocity
**Bearings → components**: bearing $\theta$ ⇒ $\mathbf{v} = v\sin\theta\,\mathbf{i} + v\cos\theta\,\mathbf{j}$ (i = east, j = north)
rows.why: 🔴 Phuc said "anti-clockwise from N" — bearings are CLOCKWISE from N. 6 redos in this area

rows.formula: Bearings convention: clockwise from North; range 000°–360°
Magnitude: $\
Position at time $t$ (constant velocity): $\mathbf{r}(t) = \mathbf{r}_0 + \mathbf{v}t$
Relative position: $\overrightarrow{AB}(t) = \mathbf{r}_B(t) - \mathbf{r}_A(t)$
**Collision condition**: $\overrightarrow{AB}(t) = \mathbf{0}$ at the same $t$ for both i and j components
**Closest approach**: minimise $\
Bearing of B from A = bearing of vector $\overrightarrow{AB}$, measured clockwise from N
Direction of $\mathbf{v}$ as bearing: $\theta = \arctan(v_x/v_y)$ adjusted for quadrant
Unit vector: $\hat{\mathbf{v}} = \mathbf{v}/\
**Newton's 2nd law**: $\mathbf{F}_{\text{net}} = m\mathbf{a}$ (apply per direction)
Weight: $W = mg$ (acts vertically downward)
Newton's 3rd law: action–reaction pairs are equal magnitude, opposite direction, same type, on different bodies
Connected particles (light inextensible string): **same acceleration** (magnitude); tension uniform along string
rows.why: 🔴 Phuc's Q3 redo Paper 1 — string concept

rows.formula: Two-particle pulley (Atwood): $a = \dfrac{(m_1-m_2)g}{m_1+m_2}$, $T = \dfrac{2m_1m_2 g}{m_1+m_2}$
rows.why: Memorize result for sanity-check; show working from F=ma in exam

rows.formula: Particle on table + hanging mass: $a = \dfrac{m_2 g}{m_1+m_2}$ (smooth) or $a = \dfrac{(m_2-\mu m_1)g}{m_1+m_2}$ (rough)
Tow-bar / car+trailer: whole system $D - R_{\text{tot}} = (M_c+M_t)a$; trailer alone $T - R_t = M_t a$
Lift / elevator: accelerating up ⇒ $R - mg = ma$ (R > mg); accelerating down ⇒ $mg - R = ma$ (R < mg)
Force diagram protocol: draw EACH particle separately, label all forces incl weight, normal R, tension T, friction F
**Inclined plane** (angle $\alpha$): weight components are $mg\sin\alpha$ down-slope and $mg\cos\alpha$ into slope; $R = mg\cos\alpha$
**Friction**: $F \le \mu R$ (general); $F = \mu R$ at limiting equilibrium / when sliding
Friction direction: opposes motion (or tendency to motion)
**Angled rope pulling UP** at angle $\theta$ to horizontal: $R = mg - T\sin\theta$, so $F = \mu(mg - T\sin\theta)$
rows.why: 🔴 Phuc's Q6 Paper 1 — rope reduces R

rows.formula: **Angled push DOWN** at angle $\theta$ below horizontal: $R = mg + P\sin\theta$, so $F = \mu(mg + P\sin\theta)$
Resolving general force at angle $\theta$ to horizontal: horizontal component $F\cos\theta$, vertical $F\sin\theta$
On inclined plane being pushed UP (force $P$ parallel to slope): $P - mg\sin\alpha - \mu mg\cos\alpha = ma$
On inclined plane sliding DOWN (no applied force): $mg\sin\alpha - \mu mg\cos\alpha = ma$
**Angle of friction** $\lambda$: $\tan\lambda = \mu$; an object on rough incline slides if $\alpha > \lambda$
Lami's theorem (3 forces in equilibrium): $\dfrac{F_1}{\sin\alpha_1} = \dfrac{F_2}{\sin\alpha_2} = \dfrac{F_3}{\sin\alpha_3}$ ($\alpha_i$ opposite $F_i$)
**Momentum**: $p = mv$ (vector; units N·s or kg·m/s)
**Conservation of momentum**: $m_1 u_1 + m_2 u_2 = m_1 v_1 + m_2 v_2$
**Impulse** = change in momentum: $\mathbf{I} = m\mathbf{v} - m\mathbf{u} = \Delta \mathbf{p}$
rows.why: "Find impulse on A" — Phuc clean here

rows.formula: Impulse from constant force: $\mathbf{I} = \mathbf{F}t$ (use when force × time given)
Newton's 3rd law on impulse: impulse on A by B = $-$impulse on B by A
Coalescence (perfectly inelastic): $m_1 u_1 + m_2 u_2 = (m_1 + m_2)v$
Vector impulse: apply per-component (i and j separately), magnitude $\sqrt{I_x^2 + I_y^2}$
**Direction reversal**: take a sign convention; if final velocity is negative, particle reversed direction
Equilibrium: $\sum F_x = 0$ AND $\sum F_y = 0$ (and $a=0$)
Triangle of forces: 3 forces in equilibrium form a closed triangle (head-to-tail) — solve via sine/cosine rule
**Cosine rule** (2 sides + included angle): $a^2 = b^2 + c^2 - 2bc\cos A$
rows.why: 🔴 Phuc forgot this on Paper 1 Q2

rows.formula: **Sine rule**: $\dfrac{a}{\sin A} = \dfrac{b}{\sin B} = \dfrac{c}{\sin C}$
Parallelogram of forces (resultant): $\
Ring on string / bead on wire: smooth ⇒ tension same both sides, ring at lowest point of string
Ring/bead on rough rod, min/max friction — friction direction flips between min and max cases
**Moment** = force × perpendicular distance: $M = Fd$ (units Nm)
**Equilibrium of rigid body**: $\sum F = 0$ AND $\sum M = 0$ about any point
$\sum(\text{clockwise moments}) = \sum(\text{anticlockwise moments})$
**Pivot selection rule**: take moments about a point where an unknown force acts to eliminate it
rows.why: 🔴 Phuc's pivot strategy was confused — Paper 1 Q4

rows.formula: **"On point of tilting about X"** ⇒ reaction at all OTHER supports = 0
Moment of force at angle $\theta$ to rod: $M = F \cdot d \cdot \sin\theta$ where $d$ is along the rod (use perpendicular component of force)
Uniform rod: weight acts at midpoint (length $L$ ⇒ at $L/2$ from either end)
Non-uniform rod: weight position must be **given or found** (not midpoint)
Resolve vertically AND take moments — two equations, two unknowns
"On point of overturning/tilting": only one support touches; condition gives critical mass/distance
Sign convention for moments: pick clockwise or anticlockwise as positive at start; **state it**
rows.why: 🔴 Phuc had sign errors Paper 3 Q1

units.top10:
1. **Bearing → components**: $i = v\sin(\text{bearing})$, $j = v\cos(\text{bearing})$. Bearings are CLOCKWISE from North. 🔴 6 redos.
2. **Moments equilibrium**: $\sum$cw $=\sum$acw, AND pivot strategy: take moments about the unknown to eliminate it. 🔴 7 redos.
3. **"On point of tilting about X"** ⇒ reaction at all other supports = 0. 🔴
4. **Friction with angled rope UP**: $R = mg - T\sin\theta$, $F = \mu(mg - T\sin\theta)$. 🔴
5. **Friction direction**: opposes motion. Limiting ⇒ $F = \mu R$. Min/max problems flip direction. 🔴
6. **Inclined plane**: $R = mg\cos\alpha$, weight component down-slope $= mg\sin\alpha$.
7. **SUVAT five**: $v=u+at$, $s=ut+\tfrac12 at^2$, $v^2=u^2+2as$, $s=\tfrac12(u+v)t$, $s=vt-\tfrac12 at^2$.
8. **Two-phase motion**: end conditions of phase 1 = initial of phase 2; new $a$ each phase.
9. **Closest approach**: differentiate $|\mathbf{d}(t)|^2$, NOT $|\mathbf{d}(t)|$.
10. **Newton's 2nd law per particle** + connected (inextensible) string ⇒ **same** $a$.

chapters.title: 6 — Transformations Using Matrices

rows.formula: Reflection in $x$-axis: $\begin{pmatrix}1&0\\0&-1\end{pmatrix}$
**Reflection in $y$-axis**: $\begin{pmatrix}-1&0\\0&1\end{pmatrix}$
Reflection in $y=x$: $\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}$;  in $y=-x$: $\begin{pmatrix}0&-1\\-1&0\end{pmatrix}$

chapters.title: 8 — Proof by Induction

rows.formula: **4-step template**: (1) Base $n=1$: LHS=RHS;  (2) Assume true for $n=k$;  (3) Show true for $n=k+1$;  (4) Conclude — *"True for $n=1$. If true for $n=k$ then true for $n=k+1$. Therefore by induction, true for all positive integers $n$."*
Divisibility trick: $f(k+1) - mf(k) = $ something obviously divisible by the target. Then $f(k+1) = mf(k) + (\text{divisible thing})$
Matrix-power induction: assume $\mathbf{M}^k = $ given form, then $\mathbf{M}^{k+1} = \mathbf{M}^k \cdot \mathbf{M}$ — multiply and simplify
Recurrence-relation induction: from $u_{k+1} = g(u_k)$ + assumed closed form for $u_k$ → derive closed form for $u_{k+1}$

chapters.in_booklet:
- *None — induction is purely a method, not a formula.*

4. **$\sum_{r=1}^n r = \tfrac{1}{2}n(n+1)$** — NOT in booklet (only $r^2, r^3$ are). 🔴

$\sec\theta = \dfrac{1}{\cos\theta},\quad \csc\theta = \dfrac{1}{\sin\theta},\quad \cot\theta = \dfrac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta} = \dfrac{1}{\tan\theta}$

chapters.title: 4 — Trig Addition & Double-Angle (compound angle in booklet, double angle NOT)

rows.formula: $\sin 2A = 2\sin A \cos A$
$\cos 2A = \cos^2 A - \sin^2 A = 2\cos^2 A - 1 = 1 - 2\sin^2 A$ — three forms

**Pattern recognition**: $(\cos\theta + \sin\theta)(\cos\theta - \sin\theta) = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta = \cos 2\theta$
**R-formula**: $a\sin x + b\cos x = R\sin(x + \alpha)$, $R = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2}$, $\tan\alpha = \tfrac{b}{a}$
R-formula variants: $a\sin x - b\cos x = R\sin(x - \alpha)$; $a\cos x + b\sin x = R\cos(x - \alpha)$; $a\cos x - b\sin x = R\cos(x + \alpha)$. Always $R = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2}$, $\tan\alpha = b/a$
Max of $R\sin(x + \alpha) + c$ is $R + c$ at $x + \alpha = \tfrac{\pi}{2}$; min is $-R + c$ at $x + \alpha = -\tfrac{\pi}{2}$
"Second smallest positive $x$ at which max occurs": max at $x + \alpha = \tfrac{\pi}{2} + 2n\pi$. Take $n = 1$ for second smallest

chapters.title: 6 — Differentiation (chain, product, quotient, standard)

rows.formula: **Chain rule**: $\dfrac{dy}{dx} = \dfrac{dy}{du}\cdot\dfrac{du}{dx}$ — equivalently $(f \circ g)' = f'(g)\cdot g'$
**Product rule**: $(uv)' = u'v + uv'$
$\dfrac{d}{dx}(\sin kx) = k\cos kx$

$\dfrac{d}{dx}(e^{kx}) = ke^{kx}$

$\dfrac{d}{dx}(\ln(f(x))) = \dfrac{f'(x)}{f(x)}$ (chain rule combo)
$\dfrac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x \ln a$
$\dfrac{d}{dx}(e^{f(x)}) = f'(x)\,e^{f(x)}$ — chain rule on exponential
$\dfrac{d}{dx}((ax + b)^{-1}) = -a(ax + b)^{-2}$ — chain rule on reciprocal
"Turning points": solve $f'(x) = 0$. Reject impossible solutions ($\sin x > 1$ etc.)

rows.formula: $\displaystyle \int \dfrac{f'(x)}{f(x)}\,dx = \ln\lvert f(x) \rvert + C$ — recognise the pattern

rows.formula: **Sign change theorem**: if $f$ continuous on $[a,b]$ and $f(a)\cdot f(b) < 0$, then $\exists\, \alpha \in (a,b)$ with $f(\alpha) = 0$
Mark scheme phrasing: "$f$ is a polynomial, hence continuous" + "$f(a) < 0$ and $f(b) > 0$" + "by IVT, root in $(a,b)$"
rows.why: Apr 18 Q2 — Mr Fuat: take simple integers

rows.formula: Iteration: rearrange $f(x) = 0$ to $x = g(x)$, then $x_{n+1} = g(x_n)$
"Act with respect to the answer": if target involves $\sqrt[3]{\cdot/x}$, divide by $x$ before cube-rooting
Convergence condition: iteration $x = g(x)$ converges near root $\alpha$ when $\lvert g'(\alpha) \rvert < 1$
**Calculator workflow**: type $x_1$ then $=$ (now ANS = $x_1$). Type $g(\text{ANS})$ then $=$ repeatedly
Stop iterating when consecutive values agree to required d.p.
**Failure of change-of-sign**: if $f$ has a discontinuity in $[a, b]$ (e.g. $1/(x-c)$) or even number of roots, sign change can mislead

units.top10:
1. **R-formula** $a\sin x + b\cos x = R\sin(x + \alpha)$, $R = \sqrt{a^2+b^2}$, $\tan\alpha = b/a$ — forgotten in **2 papers**, ⭐ Apr 18 Q9 was Phuc's highest-priority redo. Drill all 4 variants (sin±, cos±).
2. **Double-angle identities** all forms — $\sin 2A = 2\sin A\cos A$, $\cos 2A$ in three forms, plus reverse $\cos^2 A = \tfrac{1}{2}(1+\cos 2A)$. NOT given. Forgotten twice.
3. **$\int \tfrac{1}{ax+b}\,dx = \tfrac{1}{a}\ln\lvert ax+b\rvert + C$** — Phuc's #1 integration mistake. Forgets the $\tfrac{1}{a}$. Drill until automatic.
4. **Log linearisation both directions**: $y = kx^n \to$ line on $\log y / \log x$; $\log_a N = mT + c \to N = a^c \cdot (a^m)^T$. Year-1 gap, hit twice.
5. **$\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$** plus **$1 + \tan^2 = \sec^2$** and **$1 + \cot^2 = \csc^2$** — NOT given. Foundation for every P3 trig manipulation.
6. **Take $\ln$ when the unknown is in the exponent** — chronic. Always. For decay, growth, modelling: $a^T = b \to T = \tfrac{\ln b}{\ln a}$.
7. **Product rule $(uv)' = u'v + uv'$** plus **chain rule $\tfrac{d}{dx}(e^{f(x)}) = f'(x)e^{f(x)}$** — Apr 18 Q6 needed both.
8. **Two-modulus / one-modulus case selection**: $\lvert f \rvert = f$ or $-f$ depending on sign of $f$, AND check each solution lies in the case region. Read off graph.
9. **"If product = 0 and one factor can't be zero, the other must be zero"** — Phuc's own learned principle. Exponential is always positive, so factor it out and set the rest to zero.
10. **Sign-change theorem mark-scheme phrasing** + **iteration ANS-button workflow**. Use simple integer endpoints; cite continuity; stop when d.p. stabilise.

rows.formula: Apply initial conditions to find constant of integration $C$ — show working in **log form before exponentiating**
Exponential decay/growth template: solving $\dfrac{dx}{dt}=k(A-x)$ → $x=A(1-e^{-kt})$, limit $A$ as $t\to\infty$
$\displaystyle\int \sin^2 x\,dx = \tfrac{1}{2}x - \tfrac{1}{4}\sin 2x + C$, $\displaystyle\int \cos^2 x\,dx = \tfrac{1}{2}x + \tfrac{1}{4}\sin 2x + C$ (use double-angle)
rows.why: Comes up in volume-of-revolution with $y=\sin x$

chapters.in_booklet:
- $\displaystyle\int \sec^2 kx\,dx = \tfrac{1}{k}\tan kx + C$ — given
- $\displaystyle\int \tan x\,dx = \ln\|\sec x\| + C$ — given
- $\displaystyle\int \cot x\,dx = \ln\|\sin x\| + C$ — given
- $\displaystyle\int \csc x\,dx = -\ln\|\csc x + \cot x\| + C$ — given
- $\displaystyle\int \sec x\,dx = \ln\|\sec x + \tan x\| + C$ — given
- IBP formula: $\displaystyle\int u\,\dfrac{dv}{dx}\,dx = uv - \int v\,\dfrac{du}{dx}\,dx$ — **given** (still memorize for fluency)

---

chapters.title: 7 — Vectors (3D)

rows.formula: Magnitude (3D): $\
Position vector: $\overrightarrow{AB} = \mathbf{b} - \mathbf{a}$ where $\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}$ are position vectors of $A, B$
Distance between two 3D points: $\sqrt{(x_1-x_2)^2+(y_1-y_2)^2+(z_1-z_2)^2}$
rows.why: "Find the distance from A to B"

rows.formula: Unit vector in direction of $\mathbf{a}$: $\hat{\mathbf{a}} = \dfrac{\mathbf{a}}{\
**Scalar (dot) product**: $\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b} = a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3$
rows.why: Used to find angle, perpendicularity, projection

rows.formula: Angle between vectors: $\cos\theta = \dfrac{\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}}{\
**Perpendicularity test**: $\mathbf{a}\perp\mathbf{b} \iff \mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b} = 0$
rows.why: Phuc was clean on this Mar 2026 Q2 — keep

rows.formula: Properties: $\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{a} = \
**Vector equation of line** through $\mathbf{a}$ in direction $\mathbf{b}$: $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a} + \lambda\mathbf{b}$
Line through two points $C, D$: $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{c} + \lambda(\mathbf{d}-\mathbf{c})$
Lines intersect: equate components, solve 2 of 3 simultaneously, **check 3rd component** to confirm
Parallel lines: direction vectors are scalar multiples ($\mathbf{b}_1 = k\mathbf{b}_2$)
Skew lines: not parallel AND don't intersect (system inconsistent in 3rd component)
Section formula: $P$ divides $AB$ in ratio $\lambda:\mu \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{OP} = \overrightarrow{OA} + \dfrac{\lambda}{\lambda+\mu}(\overrightarrow{OB}-\overrightarrow{OA})$
Foot of perpendicular from point $P$ to line $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a}+\lambda\mathbf{b}$: solve $(\mathbf{r}-\mathbf{p})\cdot\mathbf{b}=0$ for $\lambda$
Shortest distance from point to line: $\
Area of triangle from vectors: $\tfrac{1}{2}\
Three points collinear: $\overrightarrow{AB}$ and $\overrightarrow{AC}$ are parallel

chapters.in_booklet:
- (none — vectors get no booklet support; this is the largest must-memorize block)

---

units.top10:
1. **Binomial $(a+bx)^n$ factor-out + validity** — `(a+bx)^n = a^n(1+bx/a)^n`, valid for `|x|<|a/b|`. 🔴 Phuc lost marks on this Mar 2026.
2. **Index rule $(a^m)^n = a^{mn}$** — specifically: $8^{4/3}=16$, not 2. 🔴 Direct cause of Q7 error.
3. **Parametric area** — $\int y\,(dx/dt)\,dt$, and the x-width is $\int (dx/dt)\,dt$, **not** $t_2-t_1$. 🔴
4. **Implicit differentiation rules** — $\dfrac{d}{dx}(y^n)=ny^{n-1}\dfrac{dy}{dx}$, product rule $\dfrac{d}{dx}(xy)=x\dfrac{dy}{dx}+y$.
5. **Separation of variables** — $\int\dfrac{1}{g(y)}\,dy = \int f(x)\,dx$, then apply ICs.
6. **Dot product + angle**: $\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}=a_1b_1+a_2b_2+a_3b_3$; $\cos\theta = \mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}/(\|\mathbf{a}\|\|\mathbf{b}\|)$.
7. **Vector line equation**: $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a} + \lambda\mathbf{b}$ — and the intersect-and-check-3rd-component method.
8. **Partial fractions decomposition** (distinct + repeated) → integrate each as $\frac{1}{a}\ln|ax+b|$.
9. **Connected rates of change**: $\dfrac{dA}{dt}=\dfrac{dA}{dr}\cdot\dfrac{dr}{dt}$.
10. **Volume of revolution**: $V = \pi\int y^2\,dx$ (Cartesian) or $\pi\int y^2\,(dx/dt)\,dt$ (parametric).

units.name: Further Pure 2
units.headline:
- Total formulas needed: ~52
- Given in booklet: 9 (polar area, Euler's formula, de Moivre, $n$th roots of unity, Maclaurin/Taylor general forms, $e^x$, $\ln(1+x)$, $\sin x$, $\cos x$, $\arctan x$ standard expansions)
- **Must memorize: 43**
- Priority breakdown: P0=15, P1=18, P2=8, P3=2

> **No past papers attempted yet.** Priority is anchored in: (a) textbook emphasis, (b) what's NOT in the booklet (everything not in booklet is auto-P1 minimum), (c) common Edexcel exam patterns.

chapters.title: 1 — Inequalities

rows.formula: When multiplying/dividing an inequality by an expression, you must know its **sign**. If sign unknown, **multiply by the square** (always positive) — e.g., for $\dfrac{f(x)}{g(x)} > k$, multiply by $g(x)^2$.
Critical points of $\dfrac{f(x)}{g(x)} > k$ are where $f(x) = k\,g(x)$ AND where $g(x) = 0$ (vertical asymptotes)
rows.why: Standard method, easy to drop asymptote

rows.formula: Modulus inequality: $\
Method by sketch: graph $y = f(x)$ and $y = k$; read off intervals where $f(x) > k$
rows.why: Often quickest if curve is sketchable

chapters.in_booklet:
- *None* — inequalities give no formulas.

---

chapters.title: 2 — Series (Method of Differences)

rows.formula: **Method of differences**: if $u_r = f(r) - f(r+1)$ then $\sum_{r=1}^n u_r = f(1) - f(n+1)$ (telescoping)
rows.why: Core technique — most "show that" series Qs

rows.formula: Standard partial-fraction split for telescoping: $\dfrac{1}{r(r+1)} = \dfrac{1}{r} - \dfrac{1}{r+1}$
$\dfrac{1}{r(r+2)} = \dfrac{1}{2}\left(\dfrac{1}{r} - \dfrac{1}{r+2}\right)$;  $\dfrac{1}{r(r+1)(r+2)} = \dfrac{1}{2}\left(\dfrac{1}{r(r+1)} - \dfrac{1}{(r+1)(r+2)}\right)$
After telescoping, **always check** which terms survive — typically only the first $f(1)$ and last $f(n+1)$ remain

chapters.in_booklet:
- *None* — method of differences is a method, not a formula.

---

chapters.title: 3 — Complex Numbers (Exponential Form & De Moivre)

rows.formula: Modulus-argument form: $z = r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta)$
$z_1 z_2$: multiply moduli, **add** arguments. $z_1/z_2$: divide moduli, **subtract** arguments
De Moivre: applies for **any integer $n$** including negative (booklet states for $n$, but make sure you use it for $n=-1, -2$ etc.)
rows.why: Booklet gives the formula but not the integer-range subtleties

rows.formula: Useful: $z + \dfrac{1}{z} = 2\cos\theta$,  $z - \dfrac{1}{z} = 2i\sin\theta$,  $z^n + \dfrac{1}{z^n} = 2\cos n\theta$,  $z^n - \dfrac{1}{z^n} = 2i\sin n\theta$
Method to express $\cos^n\theta$ (or $\sin^n\theta$) as sum of $\cos k\theta$/$\sin k\theta$: write $\cos\theta = \tfrac{1}{2}(z+z^{-1})$, expand binomially, group
Method to expand $\cos n\theta$ as polynomial in $\cos\theta$: use $(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta)^n$ and equate real parts
$n$th roots of any complex number $w = R e^{i\phi}$: $z_k = R^{1/n} e^{i(\phi + 2\pi k)/n}$ for $k = 0,1,\dots,n-1$
Roots of $z^n = w$ form a regular $n$-gon on circle radius $R^{1/n}$ centred at origin, equally spaced by $2\pi/n$

chapters.in_booklet:
- $e^{i\theta} = \cos\theta + i\sin\theta$
- $\{r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta)\}^n = r^n(\cos n\theta + i\sin n\theta)$
- Roots of $z^n = 1$: $z = e^{2\pi ik/n}$, $k=0,1,\dots,n-1$

---

chapters.title: 4 — Further Argand Diagram (Loci, Regions, Transformations)

rows.formula: $\arg(z - z_0) = \theta$ → **half-line** from $z_0$ at angle $\theta$ to positive real axis (excluding $z_0$ itself)
$\arg\left(\dfrac{z-z_1}{z-z_2}\right) = \theta$ → **arc** of a circle through $z_1$ and $z_2$
Region inequalities: $\
Transformation $w = f(z)$: substitute $z = $ inverse map into the locus equation in $z$ to get the locus in $w$
rows.why: Standard FP2 exam style — Möbius-type maps

chapters.in_booklet:
- *None* for loci/transformations.

---

chapters.title: 5 — First-Order Differential Equations

rows.formula: Separable: $\dfrac{dy}{dx} = f(x)g(y) \Rightarrow \int \dfrac{dy}{g(y)} = \int f(x)\,dx + C$
Linear: $\dfrac{dy}{dx} + P(x)\,y = Q(x)$
**Integrating factor**: $I(x) = e^{\int P(x)\,dx}$
After multiplying by $I$: $\dfrac{d}{dx}\big[I(x)\,y\big] = I(x)\,Q(x)$, then integrate both sides
rows.why: LHS is always $(I \cdot y)'$ — saves time

rows.formula: Substitution to reduce: e.g., $z = y/x$ for homogeneous, or $u = $ given expression — differentiate $u$, sub into ODE, solve in $u$, back-sub

chapters.in_booklet:
- *None* — no first-order DE formulas given. Integrating factor especially must be memorized.

---

chapters.title: 6 — Second-Order Differential Equations

rows.formula: **Auxiliary equation** for $a y'' + b y' + c y = 0$: $am^2 + bm + c = 0$
Case 1, $b^2 > 4ac$ (real distinct roots $\alpha, \beta$): $y = Ae^{\alpha x} + Be^{\beta x}$
Case 2, $b^2 = 4ac$ (repeated root $\alpha$): $y = (A + Bx)e^{\alpha x}$
Case 3, $b^2 < 4ac$ (complex roots $p \pm qi$): $y = e^{px}(A\cos qx + B\sin qx)$
**Non-homogeneous** $a y'' + by' + cy = f(x)$: general solution $= $ complementary function (CF) + particular integral (PI)
PI trial forms: $f = p$ → try $\lambda$;  $f=p+qx$ → $\lambda+\mu x$;  $f = pe^{kx}$ → $\lambda e^{kx}$;  $f = p\cos\omega x + q\sin\omega x$ → $\lambda \cos\omega x + \mu \sin\omega x$
rows.why: The crux — must memorize all five forms

rows.formula: If trial PI is already a term of CF, **multiply trial by $x$** (or $x^2$ if double-root clash)
Boundary/initial conditions: apply AFTER writing general solution, to determine $A$ and $B$
Substitution to reduce 2nd-order: e.g., $x = e^t$ converts Cauchy–Euler to constant-coefficient

chapters.in_booklet:
- *None* — all second-order DE methods must be memorized.

---

chapters.title: 7 — Maclaurin and Taylor Series

rows.formula: Method to find Maclaurin/Taylor series of $f$: compute $f(0), f'(0), f''(0), \dots$ then plug into the booklet formula
rows.why: The formula is given but the *method* isn't

rows.formula: Series solutions of ODEs: assume $y(x) = \sum a_n x^n$, differentiate, sub into ODE, equate coefficients
Differentiating both sides of an implicit equation repeatedly and substituting $x=0$ to find $f^{(r)}(0)$
rows.why: Standard FP2 trick when $f$ defined implicitly

rows.formula: Compose series: substitute one Maclaurin into another — e.g. $e^{\sin x}$ — and keep terms up to required order
Useful: $\tan x = x + \tfrac{x^3}{3} + \tfrac{2x^5}{15} + \dots$;  $(1+x)^n = 1 + nx + \tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}x^2 + \dots$ (binomial, $\

chapters.in_booklet:
- General Maclaurin: $f(x) = f(0) + xf'(0) + \tfrac{x^2}{2!}f''(0) + \dots$
- General Taylor about $a$: $f(x) = f(a) + (x-a)f'(a) + \tfrac{(x-a)^2}{2!}f''(a) + \dots$
- $f(a+x) = f(a) + xf'(a) + \tfrac{x^2}{2!}f''(a) + \dots$
- $e^x$, $\ln(1+x)$, $\sin x$, $\cos x$, $\arctan x$ standard expansions all given.
- ⚠️ **$\tan x$ and binomial $(1+x)^n$ are NOT given** — memorize.

---

chapters.title: 8 — Polar Coordinates

rows.formula: Conversion: $x = r\cos\theta$,  $y = r\sin\theta$,  $r^2 = x^2+y^2$,  $\tan\theta = y/x$ (quadrant!)
$r = a$ → circle radius $a$ centred at $O$;  $\theta = \alpha$ → half-line at angle $\alpha$
$r = 2a\cos\theta$ → circle through $O$, centre $(a,0)$, radius $a$
**Cardioid**: $r = a(1 + \cos\theta)$ — heart shape, max $r=2a$ at $\theta=0$, $r=0$ at $\theta=\pi$
**Rose**: $r = a\cos n\theta$ — $n$ petals if $n$ odd, $2n$ petals if $n$ even
**Limaçon**: $r = a + b\cos\theta$ — inner loop iff $a < b$, dimple iff $a < 2b$, convex iff $a \ge 2b$
Area between two curves: $A = \tfrac{1}{2}\int_\alpha^\beta (r_1^2 - r_2^2)\,d\theta$
**Limits of integration are angles** — find them by setting $r=0$ (curve through origin) or $r_1 = r_2$ (intersections)
Tangent at the pole: occurs where $r = 0$;  the tangent line is $\theta = $ that value
Horizontal tangent: $\dfrac{dy}{d\theta} = 0$ where $y = r\sin\theta$;  vertical tangent: $\dfrac{dx}{d\theta} = 0$ where $x = r\cos\theta$
For "rose" curve: integrate over **one petal** ($r \ge 0$) and multiply by number of petals to get total area

chapters.in_booklet:
- Area of polar sector: $A = \tfrac{1}{2}\int r^2\,d\theta$

---

units.top10:
1. **Auxiliary equation + 3 cases** (real distinct, repeated, complex) — entire 2nd-order ODE family. NOT in booklet.
2. **PI trial forms table** + the "multiply by $x$ if clash with CF" rule.
3. **Integrating factor** $I = e^{\int P\,dx}$ for $\dfrac{dy}{dx} + Py = Q$. NOT in booklet.
4. **De Moivre tricks** — $z + 1/z = 2\cos\theta$, $z - 1/z = 2i\sin\theta$, and the $\cos^n\theta / \cos n\theta$ expansion methods.
5. **$n$th roots of any complex number**: $z_k = R^{1/n} e^{i(\phi + 2\pi k)/n}$ — generalises the booklet's $z^n = 1$ formula.
6. **Polar area between two curves**: $A = \tfrac{1}{2}\int (r_1^2 - r_2^2)\,d\theta$ — booklet only gives the single-curve version.
7. **Method of differences** + standard partial-fraction telescope $\dfrac{1}{r(r+1)} = \dfrac{1}{r} - \dfrac{1}{r+1}$.
8. **Loci on Argand**: circle $\|z-z_0\|=r$;  perpendicular bisector $\|z-z_1\|=\|z-z_2\|$;  half-line $\arg(z-z_0)=\theta$;  arc $\arg\left(\dfrac{z-z_1}{z-z_2}\right)=\theta$.
9. **Inequality method**: multiply by $g(x)^2$ when sign unknown; critical points include vertical asymptotes ($g=0$).
10. **Implicit Maclaurin technique**: differentiate the defining equation repeatedly, sub $x=0$ to extract $f^{(r)}(0)$.

units.name: Further Pure 3
units.headline:
- Total formulas needed: ~58
- Given in booklet: ~28 (hyperbolic identities + log forms, all conics with foci/directrices/asymptotes, all inverse-trig & hyperbolic derivatives/integrals, vector product in component form, scalar triple product, plane equations, perpendicular distance, arc length, surface area)
- **Must memorize: 30**
- Priority breakdown: P0=11, P1=13, P2=5, P3=1

> **No past papers attempted yet.** Priority is anchored in: (a) textbook emphasis, (b) what's NOT in the booklet (everything not in booklet is auto-P1 minimum). The FP3 booklet gives a lot, so this list is METHODS + APPLICATIONS + 3×3 matrix work that's entirely absent from the booklet.

chapters.title: 1 — Hyperbolic Functions

rows.formula: $\sinh x = \dfrac{e^x - e^{-x}}{2}$,  $\cosh x = \dfrac{e^x + e^{-x}}{2}$,  $\tanh x = \dfrac{\sinh x}{\cosh x} = \dfrac{e^{2x}-1}{e^{2x}+1}$
rows.why: NOT in booklet — definitions are fundamental

rows.formula: Reciprocal hyperbolics: $\text{sech } x = 1/\cosh x$,  $\text{cosech } x = 1/\sinh x$,  $\coth x = 1/\tanh x$
Symmetry: $\sinh(-x) = -\sinh x$ (odd),  $\cosh(-x) = \cosh x$ (even),  $\tanh(-x) = -\tanh x$
Derived identity: $1 - \tanh^2 x \equiv \text{sech}^2 x$ (divide booklet's $\cosh^2 - \sinh^2 = 1$ by $\cosh^2 x$)
$\cosh^2 x = \tfrac{1}{2}(\cosh 2x + 1)$,  $\sinh^2 x = \tfrac{1}{2}(\cosh 2x - 1)$ (rearrangements of booklet's $\cosh 2x$)

**Osborn's rule**: every trig identity holds for hyperbolics with one sign flip per *product of two sinh's*. E.g., $\cos(A+B) = \cos A\cos B - \sin A\sin B$ becomes $\cosh(A+B) = \cosh A\cosh B + \sinh A\sinh B$
rows.why: Quickest way to derive any hyperbolic identity not listed

rows.formula: Method to solve $a\cosh x + b\sinh x = c$ (or $\sinh^2$, $\cosh^2$ equations): substitute $u = e^x$ → quadratic in $u$, then $x = \ln u$

chapters.in_booklet:
- $\cosh^2 x - \sinh^2 x \equiv 1$
- $\sinh 2x \equiv 2\sinh x\cosh x$
- $\cosh 2x \equiv \cosh^2 x + \sinh^2 x$
- $\text{arcosh } x = \ln\{x + \sqrt{x^2-1}\}$ ($x \ge 1$)
- $\text{arsinh } x = \ln\{x + \sqrt{x^2+1}\}$
- $\text{artanh } x = \tfrac{1}{2}\ln\dfrac{1+x}{1-x}$ ($\|x\|<1$)

---

chapters.title: 2 — Further Coordinate Systems (Ellipse, Hyperbola)

rows.formula: **Tangent to ellipse $\dfrac{x^2}{a^2}+\dfrac{y^2}{b^2}=1$** at $(x_0, y_0)$: $\dfrac{x x_0}{a^2} + \dfrac{y y_0}{b^2} = 1$
rows.why: Booklet gives curve & parametric, NOT tangent

rows.formula: Tangent at parametric $(a\cos\theta, b\sin\theta)$: $\dfrac{x\cos\theta}{a} + \dfrac{y\sin\theta}{b} = 1$
**Tangent to hyperbola $\dfrac{x^2}{a^2} - \dfrac{y^2}{b^2} = 1$** at $(x_0, y_0)$: $\dfrac{x x_0}{a^2} - \dfrac{y y_0}{b^2} = 1$
Tangent at parametric $(a\sec\theta, b\tan\theta)$: $\dfrac{x\sec\theta}{a} - \dfrac{y\tan\theta}{b} = 1$
**Normal to ellipse** at $(x_0, y_0)$: $\dfrac{a^2 x}{x_0} - \dfrac{b^2 y}{y_0} = a^2 - b^2$
**Normal to hyperbola** at $(x_0, y_0)$: $\dfrac{a^2 x}{x_0} + \dfrac{b^2 y}{y_0} = a^2 + b^2$
Practical method: differentiate implicitly to get $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$, then $y - y_0 = m(x - x_0)$ — works for any conic, slow but safe
rows.why: Backup if stuck on explicit forms

rows.formula: Focus-directrix property: for any conic, $SP = e\cdot PM$ where $e$ is eccentricity, $M$ is foot of perpendicular to directrix

chapters.in_booklet:
- All four conics with: standard form, parametric form, eccentricity formulas $b^2 = a^2(1-e^2)$ (ellipse) / $b^2 = a^2(e^2-1)$ (hyperbola), foci, directrices, asymptotes.
- ⚠️ Booklet gives the **curves** and the **eccentricity relations** — you must memorize **tangent and normal equations**.

---

chapters.title: 3 — Differentiation

rows.formula: All standard P3/P4 rules: chain, product, quotient, implicit, parametric ($\dfrac{dy}{dx} = \dfrac{dy/dt}{dx/dt}$)
Logarithmic differentiation for $y = [f(x)]^{g(x)}$: take $\ln$ both sides, then differentiate
$\dfrac{d}{dx}[\sinh^{-1} u] = \dfrac{u'}{\sqrt{1+u^2}}$ etc. — chain rule applied to booklet derivatives (sub $u = $ inner)

chapters.in_booklet:
- $\dfrac{d}{dx}\arcsin x = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}$,  $\dfrac{d}{dx}\arccos x = -\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}$,  $\dfrac{d}{dx}\arctan x = \dfrac{1}{1+x^2}$
- $\dfrac{d}{dx}\sinh x = \cosh x$,  $\dfrac{d}{dx}\cosh x = \sinh x$,  $\dfrac{d}{dx}\tanh x = \text{sech}^2 x$
- $\dfrac{d}{dx}\text{arsinh } x = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}$,  $\dfrac{d}{dx}\text{arcosh } x = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{x^2-1}}$,  $\dfrac{d}{dx}\text{artanh } x = \dfrac{1}{1-x^2}$

---

chapters.title: 4 — Integration

rows.formula: Standard integrals NOT in booklet: $\int \tan x\,dx = \ln\
$\int \sinh x\,dx = \cosh x$,  $\int \cosh x\,dx = \sinh x$ (booklet) — but **also** $\int \tanh x\,dx = \ln(\cosh x)$ (booklet), $\int \coth x\,dx = \ln\
**Integration by parts**: $\int u\,dv = uv - \int v\,du$ — choose $u$ by **LIATE** (Log, Inverse-trig, Algebraic, Trig, Exponential)
**Reduction formula** derivation pattern: $I_n = \int (\text{stuff})^n$; integrate by parts to get $I_n$ in terms of $I_{n-1}$ or $I_{n-2}$
$I_n = \int_0^{\pi/2} \sin^n x\,dx = \dfrac{n-1}{n} I_{n-2}$ (Wallis)

Trig substitutions: for $\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}$ use $x = a\sin\theta$;  for $\sqrt{a^2 + x^2}$ use $x = a\sinh\theta$ or $x = a\tan\theta$;  for $\sqrt{x^2 - a^2}$ use $x = a\cosh\theta$ or $x = a\sec\theta$
rows.why: Tells you which method to use; booklet gives the ANSWERS but not WHEN

rows.formula: Partial fractions before integration: factorise denominator, split into $\dfrac{A}{x-a} + \dfrac{Bx+C}{x^2+\dots}$ etc., then integrate term by term
Volume of revolution: $V_x = \pi\int y^2\,dx$ (about $x$-axis), $V_y = \pi\int x^2\,dy$ (about $y$-axis)
rows.why: NOT in FP3 booklet (was P3 content) — must memorize

rows.formula: Arc length & surface area: **booklet gives the formulas** but you must know **which to apply** — Cartesian vs parametric, $S_x$ vs $S_y$ (about which axis?)
$S_y = 2\pi\int x\,ds$ (surface of revolution about $y$-axis) — **only $S_x$ is in booklet**

chapters.in_booklet:
- $\int \tanh x\,dx = \ln\cosh x$
- $\int \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}\,dx = \arcsin(x/a)$
- $\int \dfrac{1}{a^2 + x^2}\,dx = \tfrac{1}{a}\arctan(x/a)$
- $\int \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{x^2 - a^2}}\,dx = \text{arcosh}(x/a) = \ln\{x + \sqrt{x^2-a^2}\}$
- $\int \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{a^2 + x^2}}\,dx = \text{arsinh}(x/a) = \ln\{x + \sqrt{x^2+a^2}\}$
- $\int \dfrac{1}{a^2 - x^2}\,dx = \tfrac{1}{2a}\ln\dfrac{a+x}{a-x} = \tfrac{1}{a}\text{artanh}(x/a)$ ($\|x\|<a$)
- $\int \dfrac{1}{x^2 - a^2}\,dx = \tfrac{1}{2a}\ln\dfrac{x-a}{x+a}$
- Arc length $s = \int \sqrt{1 + (dy/dx)^2}\,dx$ (Cartesian) and $\int \sqrt{(dx/dt)^2 + (dy/dt)^2}\,dt$ (parametric)
- Surface of revolution about $x$-axis: $S_x = 2\pi\int y\,ds$

---

chapters.title: 5 — Vectors

rows.formula: Dot product: $\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b} = a_1 b_1 + a_2 b_2 + a_3 b_3 = \
Magnitude $\
Angle between vectors: $\cos\theta = \dfrac{\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}}{\
Perpendicular ⟺ $\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}=0$;  parallel ⟺ $\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{0}$ (or $\mathbf{a}=\lambda\mathbf{b}$)
**Area of triangle** with sides $\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}$: $\tfrac{1}{2}\
**Area of parallelogram**: $\
**Volume of parallelepiped**: $\
**Volume of tetrahedron**: $\tfrac{1}{6}\
Coplanar test: 3 vectors coplanar ⟺ scalar triple product $= 0$
**Vector equation of a line**: $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a} + \lambda\mathbf{b}$ (point + direction)
rows.why: NOT explicitly in FP3 booklet (cartesian form is) — must memorize vector form

rows.formula: **Vector equation of a plane**: $\mathbf{r}\cdot\mathbf{n} = \mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{n} = d$ (scalar product form)
rows.why: Booklet gives cartesian + parametric forms but not the dot-product form

rows.formula: Angle between two planes = angle between their normals: $\cos\theta = \dfrac{\mathbf{n}_1 \cdot \mathbf{n}_2}{\
Angle between line and plane: $\sin\theta = \dfrac{\
Distance from point $P$ to line $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{a} + \lambda\mathbf{b}$: $\dfrac{\
Shortest distance between two skew lines (with directions $\mathbf{b}_1, \mathbf{b}_2$ through points $\mathbf{a}_1, \mathbf{a}_2$): $\dfrac{\
Line of intersection of two planes: direction $= \mathbf{n}_1 \times \mathbf{n}_2$, then find a point by setting $z=0$ (say) and solving the 2 plane equations

chapters.in_booklet:
- $\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}$ in component form (i, j, k determinant); $\mathbf{a}\cdot(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})$ as $3\times 3$ determinant
- Cartesian form of a line through $\mathbf{a}$ with direction $\mathbf{b}$: $\dfrac{x-a_1}{b_1} = \dfrac{y-a_2}{b_2} = \dfrac{z-a_3}{b_3}$
- Cartesian form of a plane: $n_1 x + n_2 y + n_3 z + d = 0$ where $d = -\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{n}$
- Parametric plane forms (through 3 points; through point with two directions)
- **Perpendicular distance** of $(\alpha,\beta,\gamma)$ from $n_1 x + n_2 y + n_3 z + d = 0$: $\dfrac{\|n_1\alpha + n_2\beta + n_3\gamma + d\|}{\sqrt{n_1^2 + n_2^2 + n_3^2}}$
- ⚠️ Booklet gives a LOT for vectors — focus must-memorize on **methods/applications** (areas, volumes, angles, distances between line+line / point+line).
